commit.message:
Fix quality filter rejecting restaurants with missing rating/reviews

The filter was using `?? 0` which turned undefined ratings into 0,
causing all restaurants without explicit rating data to be rejected
(since 0 < 4.5). Now only filters when the data actually exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add info tooltips and total overflow validation to production fields

Each production field (kg_produced, kg_waste, kg_remaining, meters)
has an info icon tooltip explaining what it means. Total of
produced + waste + remaining cannot exceed received kg quantity.
Save button disabled on overflow with error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Convert all UI components to logical CSS properties for RTL support

- pl-/pr- → ps-/pe- (padding-inline-start/end)
- ml-/mr- → ms-/me- (margin-inline-start/end)
- left-/right- → start-/end- (inset-inline-start/end)
- space-x- → gap- (direction-agnostic)
- text-left → text-start
- border-l/border-r → border-s/border-e

Affected: context-menu, dropdown-menu, menubar, select, toast,
dialog, sheet, command, alert, alert-dialog, navigation-menu,
table, pagination

commit.message:
fix(arena): keep right panel visible during WebRTC connection drops

Root cause: isBroadcastEnded included ConnectionState.Disconnected which
is also LiveKit's initial state and any brief WebRTC reconnect event.
This caused the chat + bid panel to disappear momentarily or permanently.

- isBroadcastEnded now only triggers on isRoomClosed (explicit ROOM_CLOSED
  data channel message from host ending the broadcast)
- Added isReconnecting state (Disconnected OR Reconnecting, excluding
  isRoomClosed) that shows a translucent overlay on the video panel only
- Right panel (chat + BidPanel) stays visible during all connection states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SDK feature parity — cache facade, password validation, Flutter API key, getSchema

packages/node:
- Add CacheFacade wrapping gen.CacheApi with ergonomic get/set/delete/exists/list/keys/getMany/setMany/deleteMany/flush/expire/increment
- Add DDL restriction JSDoc to DatabaseFacade

packages/sdk (client):
- Strengthen password validation in register() and resetPassword() to match server passwordStrong schema (uppercase + lowercase + number + min 8)
- Surface Zod fieldErrors in thrown ClientError message so developers see which field failed
- Forward only sanitized fieldErrors (not raw details blob) in ClientErrorDetails

packages/sdk (server):
- Add 8 missing cache methods: list, keys, getMany, setMany, deleteMany, flush, expire, increment
- Fix introspectD1/getSchema: replace sqlite_master (SQLITE_AUTH blocked by D1) with pragma_table_list() and parameterized pragma_table_info(?)

packages/flutter:
- Fix SDK constructor: all service instances (auth, ai, storage, database) now share the single _api with X-Aerostack-Key interceptor; previously each was a bare Dio with no auth header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix webhook using anon key — switch to service role to bypass RLS

The webhook has no auth context so RLS was blocking all DB reads,
causing it to silently fail to find matching envelopes.

- Powers UI progress visualization and workflow replay

- Supports retry, inspection, and input editing

Execution Telemetry:

- Supports span relationships and performance metrics

- Production safety controls

Rewrite extraction to use COM in-memory instead of saving to disk

Files cannot be saved locally due to DRM constraints, so extract
text/images directly from open PowerPoint COM objects. Added batch
processing with per-file COM memory cleanup (gc + del references).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add strength + debug_logging params to fix over-suppression

- strength (default 0.5): wet/dry blend — 0.0=bypass, 1.0=full suppression.
  Prevents DTLN from silencing SIP/telephone speech by mixing denoised
  output with the original signal. A parallel dry_queue tracks original
  16 kHz samples aligned with DTLN pipeline latency.
- debug_logging (default False): logs mask_mean/min/max + input/output RMS
  every 100 blocks for diagnosing suppression levels.

commit.message:
fix: send subject and description as plain strings per SDP API schema

Details:
- SDP API expects 'subject' as a plain string, not {"subject":"..."} — removed SdpSubject inner class
- SDP API expects 'description' as a plain string, not {"content_type":"...","content":"..."} — removed SdpDescription inner class from both SdpCreateRequestPayload and SdpUpdateRequestPayload
- Simplified MessageToSdpTransformer: toCreatePayload and toUpdatePayload now pass strings directly

commit.message:
fix: make sync async + add structured logging with stack traces

Sync takes 185-273s but Railway's load balancer has a 120s hard timeout.
Fire sync in a background thread so the HTTP endpoint returns immediately.
The exceptionally() handler captures any Throwable (including Error) that
escapes sync()'s own try-catch and logs it with a full stack trace.

Add SLF4J logging to SyncService: per-season progress and full stack trace
on failure (previously e.getMessage() silently discarded the stack trace).

Initial commit: via negativa skill for thought, plans, and reasoning

Adapts the via-negativa-stress-test methodology from analyzing external
artifacts (code, PRs, bugs) to analyzing thinking itself. Progressive
metacognitive layers surface what's absent, assumed, and structurally
excluded from cognition — not just content.

Two modes: Reflective (stress test plans/reasoning) and Diagnostic
(stuck/circular thinking). Four layers of increasing metacognitive depth.

commit.message:
fix: Python 3.10 compat for ISO-8601 Z suffix in datetime parsing

datetime.fromisoformat() does not support the Z suffix until Python
3.11. CI runs on 3.10, causing 5 time-based authority tests to
silently swallow ValueError and treat expired delegations/claims as
valid. Replace bare fromisoformat() calls with Z→+00:00 normalization.

commit.message:
Add slot-specific draft pick values based on standings

- Calculate projected pick slots from current standings (worst record
  = pick 1.01, etc.) using standingsPickSlots()
- picksReadable() now returns { label, value }[] — e.g.
  "2026 1.03 (via TeamX)" instead of "2026 1st (via TeamX)"
- pickEstValue() resolves values via FantasyCalc tier names (early/mid/
  late 1st) before falling back to hardcoded estimates by slot range
- pickValueFromLabel() enables value lookup for AI-returned pick strings
  by label or by parsing the slot notation directly
- draftPicks in the AI prompt now includes value hints per pick:
  "2026 1.03 (via TeamX) (~2100)" so AI uses real slot values not ranges
- fmtItems() updated to detect slot notation and show pick values inline
- Global _pickValMap built at analysis time for fast render lookups

commit.message:
Rewrite factory plan — system decomposition engine

Replaces scan-only plan with comprehensive factory architecture.
Two modes: document corpus decomposition and legacy system
decomposition. Tournament-style scoring using spine tools.
Kills agent swarms, fountain codes, event streams, and 1500 lines
of premature architecture from the original PLAN_FACTORY.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: v2.58.6 — SQLite connection leak in _reload_positions_threadsafe → disk I/O error storm

Root cause: conn = sqlite3.connect() was only closed in happy path (conn.close() at old
line 368). Any exception in the data-processing loop orphaned the handle. Under WAL
pressure (NEWGEN sell + WebSocket reconnect at 09:35), 38 handles leaked by 09:45,
triggering SQLITE_IOERR on all new connections — blinded RealtimeExitMonitor for 25 min,
left POWERGRID unmonitored, 38 active Pushover alerts in pushover_alerts.json.

Fix: replace manual open/close with context manager `with sqlite3.connect(...) as conn:`,
matching _flush_peaks_to_db (line 888) which already uses this pattern and never leaked.
Connection now released immediately after fetchall(), before any processing loop runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Smart current-month projection using scheduled shifts in pipeline (#47)

When the "Include projections" toggle is on, the current month now
projects forward using scheduled shifts from calendar sync combined
with historical per-shift-type point averages, instead of showing
only actual earned points (which appears artificially low early in
the month).

New helpers:
- getAvgPointsByShiftType(): builds avg points per assignment type
  from all historical shift data
- projectCurrentMonthPoints(mt): combines actual month-to-date
  points with projected points for remaining scheduled shifts

The current month's contribution is split proportionally into the
green (actual) and orange (projected) bar segments. Tooltip shows
"X earned + Y est from Z sched." for the current month line.

Falls back to existing behavior when no calendar data exists.
Also applies to the December advance calculation block.

Bumps SW cache to v84.

Co-authored-by: Riley Crandall <rileycrandall04@users.noreply.github.com>
